attacking_style: Willing to commit to very long-range attacks (65km out observed) from breakaways, showing ambition over calculated patience. Operates primarily from break situations rather than peloton-based moves.
team_tactics: Limited evidence of team support; appears to operate independently in breakaway scenarios, relying on personal effort rather than domestique setup or coordinated team positioning.
terrain_preference: Breakaway stages where sustained effort over distance matters more than explosive climbing power. Struggles to convert extended climb opportunities into results when cooperation breaks down.
vulnerability: Illness has repeatedly compromised performance in Grand Tours (2 documented instances at Giro). Lacks elite finishing speed in sprint situations. Cannot sustain cooperation in fractured breakaways on extended climbs.
key_patterns: Illness/sickness derailed performance at least twice during Giro, indicating health fragility in stage races.
Makes initial breakaway selections but struggles to maintain position when effort intensifies or cooperation fails.
Demonstrates willingness for ultra-long attacks but lacks support or tactical nous to convert them.
Strong enough to lead out sprints but fades in final meters, lacking elite top-end speed.
form_trajectory: negative: Multiple instances of illness compromising breakaway efforts, with most recent 2023 Giro showing continued health-related performance issues
